name: MoE Training Optimization (Grouped GEMM)
trigger: MoE, mixture of experts, expert routing, grouped GEMM, grouped matmul, MegaBlocks, top_k experts, num_experts, expert_capacity, gate_proj, up_proj, down_proj, training, forward pass, backward pass
approach: MoE layers account for ~53% of forward-pass time in large models. Key optimizations: (1) Grouped GEMM abstraction — factor kernel into (expert_idx, row_block, col_block, reduction_start, reduction_end) and reuse core MMA logic across Fprop/Dgrad/Wgrad. (2) Expert-wise L2 supergrouping — apply ThunderKittens supergrouping per expert submatrix (not whole output matrix) to maximize L2 reuse. Bad L2 patterns cause 50% performance loss. (3) Persistent grid — assign one threadblock per SM (148 on B200) so new tiles load while results store to HBM. (4) MXFP8 quantization fusion — fuse quantization into SwiGLU/GEGLU epilogues to avoid separate quant pass. (5) Warp specialization — 3 warpgroups (384 threads): 2 for TMEM→reg→SMEM→HBM data flow, 1 for warp-specialized loads + MMA. (6) 5-slot circular buffers in TMEM and SMEM for deep pipelining. (7) For Wgrad, use cp.reduce.async.bulk.tensor for atomic accumulation.

evidence.strategy: refine kernel block sizes, tile shapes, and num_warps for optimal parallel performance
utilize hardware-native instructions and specialized tensor cores to accelerate kernel execution.
Refine block sizes, tile shapes and warp counts to further improve kernel performance.
Apply shared-memory histogram with privatized bins and warp-aggregated atomics to mitigate contention.

pitfalls: Expert-wise L2 supergrouping is critical — applying supergrouping to the whole output matrix instead of per-expert submatrix causes up to 50% performance loss
Grouped Wgrad differs from Fprop/Dgrad: expert division occurs along K (reduction) axis, not M axis — requires different loop structure
MXFP8 quantization overhead can dominate if not fused — on Blackwell, separate quant pass adds 76% to matmul time
For 2-CTA MMA on Blackwell, block-scaled mode uses only 128x256 TMEM (half) instead of full 128x512 — expect ~5% throughput drop vs non-scaled
Persistent grid requires careful synchronization — use mbarrier.try_wait for async coordination between warpgroups
DeepGEMM uses 1x128 and 128x128 scale blocks (less accurate than 32-block scaling) — 32-block MXFP8 preserves training quality
MoE forward includes both gate/up and down projections — fuse SiLU(gate) x up activation between them to avoid materializing intermediate
